messages.content: Loftwren | Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics | applied | 27 days | B+ fit | 76% match | ghost risk high | docs tailored. The posting is off their careers page and the recruiter's mailbox bounces.

applied → closed. Nobody rejected you — the req is off the page and the recruiter's mailbox bounces, so there is no one left to make a decision. You end it. Twenty-seven days at high ghost risk had already told you this. The tailored pricing docs still travel.

company: Wanderfare Group
title: Senior Manager, Revenue Science
stage: interview
days in stage: 11
fit: A
match: 90%
ghost risk: low
docs: tailored

I accepted an offer from another company an hour ago.

interview → closed, today. Rejected would mean they ended it; here you did. An A at 90% eleven days into interview is hard to hand back, so do it by phone before the next round gets booked. Continuing to interview on a signed acceptance is not a neutral act.

Forwarding the gist: Loftwren wrote back on the Senior Manager, Pricing and Analytics role saying they are moving forward with candidates whose experience more closely matches the posting. I applied 31 days ago. B+ fit, 74% match, ghost risk high, docs were tailored.

That's a decline, not a hold: applied → rejected. Thirty-one days plus that phrasing is the end of it. Keep the tailored pricing and analytics docs — at a B+ and 74% they were fine, and the framing carries over to your next revenue application.

Sablewood Resorts Group, Director of Revenue Strategy. Interview, 9 days, A- fit, 86% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored. Recruiter called this morning: comp is approved and the written offer goes out tomorrow.

Comp approved and a written offer promised tomorrow — record interview → offer so the board matches reality. Nine days in interview with low ghost risk says this ran on schedule. Do not negotiate anything verbally; wait for the document.

ok so Marrow Ridge Health, Director, Ambulatory Operations, sat in researched 11 days, B+ fit 79% match, ghost risk medium, docs not ready. a recruiter there just messaged me off a mutual contact and asked if i'd take 20 minutes this week. does that count as something

You count it. researched → conversation — an inbound ask for twenty minutes is precisely what that stage tracks, and it outweighs the medium ghost risk. Take the call before finishing docs; what you learn about the ambulatory scope will change how you tailor them.

| field | value |
|---|---|
| company | Wexley Care Partners |
| title | Operations Manager, Home Infusion |
| stage | applied |
| fit | A- |
| match | 85% |
| days in stage | 9 |
| ghost risk | low |
| docs | tailored |

HR called: the role is five days a week onsite, two states away. Moving is not possible for me.

applied → closed, not rejected — you are the one ending this. Five days onsite two states away is a constraint no A- at 85% outranks, and at nine days nobody has spent much on you yet. Withdraw in two lines so they can refill cleanly.

Verity Ridge Health Network · Clinical Informatics Analyst · conversation · 9 days · fit C · 55% · ghost risk medium · docs not started. The recruiter clarified today that it is a hands-on analyst seat with no team under it.

Take them at their word and close the loop: conversation → rejected. A hands-on seat with no team is a step sideways from managing clinical operations, and a C fit at 55% was already thin. Nine days in conversation gave you the answer you needed.

Email from Verdigris Care Network on the Senior Director, Quality and Regulatory Affairs role: they went with an internal candidate. I was at interview 18 days, B+ fit, 76% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

An internal hire ends it — interview → rejected. Nothing you send changes that outcome. Eighteen days and high ghost risk had been signaling drift already. Ask the recruiter to keep you on file for quality and regulatory openings; reaching interview at a B+ and 76% is real.
Ashgrove Mutual Insurance, Associate Pricing Analyst. sourced, 22 days, fit D, match 38%, ghost risk high, no docs. I saved this during a bad week.

sourced → rejected, without hesitation. A D fit at 38% is not a stretch, it is a mismatch, and twenty-two untouched days are the honest verdict. Clearing it means the staff-level payments roles stop competing with it for your attention.

cadenza payments, staff software engineer core ledger. still in researched, day 6, A fit, 92% match, ghost risk low, docs ready. they finally published the band and the top of it is 18% under my current base. worth applying anyway?

No — researched → closed. Tailoring cannot move a band whose top sits 18% below what you already earn, and an A at 92% makes that conversation harder, not easier: they will assume the match is enough. Six days, nothing submitted. Point the ready docs at the next ledger role.
Alderport Financial, Principal Engineer, Payments Platform. Applied 96 days ago. A- fit, 88% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored. Two follow-ups, no reply either time. The posting is still live.

applied → closed. They never rejected you and they never answered — after 96 days and two ignored follow-ups, ending it is your call, not theirs. A live posting behind a silent recruiter is not a live application. Let the 88% go and close the card.

Halberd Commerce came through with the offer for Engineering Manager, Payments. Offer stage 5 days, A- fit, 83% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored. I've decided I don't want to leave the IC track, so I'm turning it down.

Then log it honestly: offer → rejected, declined by you. A strong offer at an A- and 83% does not override a deliberate choice to stay on the IC track. Tell the recruiter inside the five days you have held it and ask them to flag staff-level openings.

Hollandale Industries · Vice President of Manufacturing Operations · researched · 13 days · A- fit · 82% match · ghost risk medium · docs half-written. Their CEO announced a hiring freeze through the end of the fiscal year on yesterday's earnings call.

researched → closed. A freeze announced on an earnings call runs to fiscal year end — longer than any card should sit waiting. Thirteen days and half-written docs make this cheap to release. Write nothing further here; re-source the role when the freeze lifts.

Brightfield Provisions, Plant Manager. I've been talking with them for 15 days now, B+ fit, 77% match, ghost risk medium, docs ready. The hiring manager said on Friday that I should formally apply through the portal so HR has a record.

When the hiring manager points you at the portal, that is the move: conversation → applied. Docs are ready, so this is a same-day task. Fifteen days of conversation at medium ghost risk means the warmth is real but expiring — submit and reference the conversation in your note.

| Palisade Converting | Production Manager, Packaging |
| stage | applied, 26 days |
| fit | B, 70% |
| ghost risk | high |
| docs | tailored |

Rejection email landed today.

Straightforward: applied → rejected. The email is explicit, and twenty-six days at high ghost risk had already told you which way this was going. A B fit at 70% was the weakest of your manufacturing applications; no rework needed, just take it off the board.

stage: researched, 5 days
company: Brightloom Foods
title: Continuous Improvement Manager
fit: B / 71%
ghost risk: medium
docs: none

The posting lists salary as TBD and gives no team size. Should this move or not?

Stay in researched for now. A B at 71% with no posted band and no team size does not give you enough to commit a director-level application to. Unlock condition: get the comp range or the reporting line from a recruiter, then it goes to applied.
Foothill Community Health Alliance / Grants Manager / researched / 13 days / C / 52% / ghost risk medium / no docs

Let it go — researched → rejected. Thirteen days and you still have not started docs, which matches a C fit at 52%. Grants management is adjacent to capital delivery, not the same job. Your transit and capital program rows deserve those hours instead.

Rivergate Port and Airport Authority, Senior Project Manager, Aviation Capital Projects. Conversation, 17 days, B+ fit, 78% match, ghost risk medium, docs ready. My current agency just handed me the capital portfolio I wanted. I do not want to keep this one warm.

conversation → closed. Your own agency gave you the portfolio, so the reason you opened this is gone — that is a withdrawal, not a rejection. Say it plainly in one message; seventeen days of goodwill is worth keeping intact in a small transit market. Then close it.
Ninebark Regional Water Authority wrote to say they've selected another finalist for the Capital Programs Director role. 24 days at interview, A fit, 89% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

Painful one, but clear: interview → rejected. A finalist decision is final. Twenty-four days in interview at high ghost risk was already a long tail. An A fit at 89% that reached finalist means the targeting is right — ask for feedback while the panel still remembers you.

| Bellrose Outfitters | Retail Operations Analyst |
| stage | researched, 8 days |
| fit | A- |
| match | 84% |
| ghost risk | low |
| docs | drafted |

They reposted it today as a nine-month contract with no benefits. I need permanent work with health coverage.

researched → closed. The job you researched no longer exists — a nine-month contract without benefits fails a requirement you cannot bend, and an A- at 84% does not buy health coverage. Close it and keep the drafted docs for the next permanent analyst posting.
bramble and rye hospitality group, operations analytics analyst. sourced 5 days, B+ fit, 79%, ghost risk low, no docs. i signed with somewhere else on friday. do i just delete these

Close them, do not delete them. sourced → closed here, and the same move on every other live card. Closing records what you targeted and how far it got; deleting erases the search. Five days in sourced with no docs written means this one costs nothing to let go.
Auto-email from Palladia Logistics: 'this position has been filled.' Business Analyst, Distribution Operations. I was at applied for 34 days, B fit, 69% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

Position filled means applied → rejected — no follow-up is going to reopen it. Thirty-four days at high ghost risk had it as good as gone already. Salvage the tailored analysis bullets; a B at 69% application still produced material you can reuse.
Kettleman Foods, Senior Vice President, Supply Chain. Researched 7 days, A fit, 90% match, ghost risk low, docs ready. An old colleague there offered to introduce me to the hiring executive.

Take the introduction first — researched → conversation. At an A fit and 90% match, a warm route in is worth more than a cold submission, and seven days of research means you can hold a real conversation. Docs stay ready for the moment it converts.
Ironbark Fulfillment · Vice President of Operations · conversation · 12 days · A- · 86% · ghost risk low · docs ready · recruiter says the req is now open and asked me to apply

The req opening is your cue: conversation → applied. Twelve days of conversation gave you the context; now make it official the same day the recruiter asked. An A- at 86% with docs ready should never be the row that stalls on paperwork.
Junipero Parcel just gave me a verbal offer on the phone for Vice President, Last-Mile Delivery, terms to follow in writing. Interview stage 16 days, A fit, 92% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

A verbal offer counts: interview → offer. Sixteen days, low ghost risk, 92% match — this went the way the signals said it would. Keep negotiating room open by thanking them warmly and saying nothing about numbers until the written terms arrive.

Tallow Creek Market Group passed on the Director of Field Operations role — said they're pursuing candidates with more field retail exposure. 29 days at applied, B fit, 66% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

applied → rejected — they named the gap, and it is not one you can argue away in an email. Twenty-nine days with high ghost risk plus a B at 66% made this the weakest logistics row you had. Nothing to salvage; put the hours into the VP-level rows.
Marrowlane Health / Vice President of Engineering / sourced / 17 days / D / 35% / high ghost risk / no docs

Cut it: sourced → rejected. Product management and engineering leadership are different jobs, which is what a D fit and a 35% match are telling you. Seventeen days without a single action is the second signal. Do not keep a row you would never actually apply to.

| company | Sunderly |
| title | Principal Product Manager, Platform APIs |
| stage | conversation (12 days) |
| fit | A |
| match | 90% |
| ghost risk | low |
| docs | ready |

The recruiter says base tops out 30k below my floor and the rest is equity. She cannot move it.

conversation → closed. She has told you the base is fixed 30k under your floor and the remainder is equity, which does not pay a mortgage. Twelve days and an A at 90% are not reasons to negotiate against a number already called final. Thank her, then close it.
Quillbase emailed after the final round: not moving forward. Director of Product, Automation. Interview 20 days, B+ fit, 78% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

Final round decided against you, so interview → rejected. Twenty days and high ghost risk were consistent with a slow no. Worth one short note asking what separated the finalists — you reached the last round at a B+ and 78%, close enough that the answer is useful.

Blue Heron Health Alliance, Chief Nursing Officer. Applied 25 days, A- fit, 85% match, ghost risk medium, docs tailored. The search firm called this afternoon to set up a first conversation with the panel.

Panel scheduling from the search firm moves this: applied → interview. Twenty-five days at medium risk was starting to look like silence, and this closes that question. Go in ready to speak to system-level nursing scope, which is what the 85% match rests on.
Harborlight Senior Living, VP of Clinical Operations. Conversation, 10 days, B fit, 72% match, ghost risk medium, no docs. They told me yesterday the role is being folded into an existing VP's remit.

If the role is being folded into someone else's remit, there is nothing to apply to: conversation → rejected. Ten days of conversation, a B at 72%, and docs never started — you lose little. Keep the contact; folded roles reopen when the remit gets too big.

Marrow Ridge Health emailed to say the Director, Ambulatory Operations position is on hold under a hiring freeze and the offer is pulled. I was at offer for 6 days, B+ fit, 76% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

A pulled offer still gets recorded: offer → rejected. A freeze is not a reflection on you — at a B+ and 76% they chose you. Six days in offer means nothing was signed, so ask to be contacted first when the freeze lifts.
Northline Coach Company, Manager, Commercial Network Planning — sourced, 6 days, B+, 79%, ghost risk low, docs not started.

Give it a research pass: sourced → researched. Six days is nothing, but a B+ at 79% with low ghost risk deserves more than a bookmark. Confirm whether the commercial network planning work is scheduling or pricing — that determines whether your docs need rebuilding at all.
Cinderpeak Resorts / Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution / researched / 16 days / C / 48% / ghost risk high / no docs

Two levels above where you are, and the numbers agree — researched → rejected. A C at 48% with high ghost risk after sixteen days of research is not going to improve by researching more. Save the stretch appetite for manager and senior manager postings.

Windrose Air Group, Manager, Network Planning. Applied 11 days ago, A fit, 88% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored. Total silence since the confirmation email and I'm starting to spiral about it. What do I do with this row?

Stay in applied. Eleven days of silence at low ghost risk is ordinary, not a warning sign, and an A fit at 88% does not need rescuing yet. It moves when they reply — or when the silence outlasts one polite follow-up. Nothing to change today.

Halewood Bancorp · Director, Regulatory Affairs · researched · 8 days · A- · 84% · ghost risk low · docs ready · a former colleague now in their compliance group offered to pass my name to the hiring director

researched → conversation, because a referral inside the compliance group beats the portal every time. Eight days of research and ready docs mean you can take that call now. Ask what the regulatory affairs mandate actually covers before you submit anything formally.
Standard rejection from Coppervane Financial on the Compliance Manager, Installment Lending role. Applied 27 days, A- fit, 86% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

Log applied → rejected. A strong row can still lose — an A- at 86% went out and came back, and twenty-seven days at high ghost risk was the tell. Worth asking the recruiter what the selected profile had; installment lending compliance is a small enough market that the answer matters.
Ardent Ledger Group just emailed the offer letter for Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance. Interview 13 days, A fit, 90% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

Offer letter in your inbox — interview → offer, and update it before you read the terms. Thirteen days from interview to paper at a 90% match and low ghost risk is a fast, clean process. Read the whole letter before replying, including the start date.

company: Kestrel Precision Components
title: Quality Engineer, Machining
stage: sourced
days: 4
fit: A
match: 87%
ghost risk: low
docs: none

Straight to sourced → researched. Same title you hold, an A fit and 87% match — the only reason it is still in sourced is that it is four days old. One evening on their machining and inspection requirements, then tailor.

Vandry Surgical, Supplier Quality Engineer II. Fourteen days in conversation, A- fit, 85% match, ghost risk low, docs ready. The recruiter said this morning that the posting is live and I should submit through it.

Recruiter told you to submit, so conversation → applied. Fourteen days of back-and-forth with docs already ready is long enough. Apply through the live posting today and email the recruiter the confirmation — an A- at 85% should not get lost in an applicant queue.
Delderfield Foods rejected me on the Controls Engineer role, specifically citing controls programming experience. Applied 23 days, C fit, 58% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

They named a specific gap in controls programming, which ends it: applied → rejected. A C fit at 58% predicted this, and twenty-three days at high ghost risk confirmed it. Keep applying inside quality engineering rather than reaching into controls, where the resume cannot back you up.

| Quillon | Head of Retention and Growth |
| stage | researched, 7 days |
| fit | A- |
| match | 88% |
| ghost risk | low |
| docs | ready |

Nothing is blocking this — researched → applied. Retention and growth is the closest match on your board at 88%, docs are ready, and seven days of research is sufficient for a director-level posting. Apply now; a low ghost risk role rewards being early.
Tessellate Software emailed that they're proceeding with another candidate for Vice President, Customer Success. I was at interview 22 days, A fit, 91% match, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

interview → rejected. A decision has been made, and twenty-two days at medium ghost risk was the run-up to it. An A at 91% that got to interview means the profile lands — ask the panel for feedback and keep the customer success search open elsewhere.

Rowanmere Health Technologies offered me the Director of Client Experience role but the base is below what I make now. Offer stage 4 days, B fit, 70% match, ghost risk low, docs tailored. I'm going to say no.

Declining is a stage change too: offer → rejected. A B fit at 70% was never the row you were building the search around, and four days is quick enough that they can move to their next candidate cleanly. Call, do not email, and say why.
Drayline Distribution, Transportation Manager, Outbound. Conversation, 9 days, A fit, 89% match, ghost risk low, docs ready. Recruiter asked me to formally apply.

conversation → applied, today. The recruiter asking for a formal application is the whole signal, and at an A fit with 89% match and ready docs there is no preparation left. Nine days of conversation gives you enough detail to reference outbound specifics in the submission.
Petrichor Beverage Company, Logistics Manager — applied 15 days, B+, 80%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored. Phone screen invite came through for next Tuesday.

Phone screen booked, so applied → interview. Fifteen days at medium ghost risk resolved in your favor. A B+ and 80% means the screen is where you close the gap — have two outbound transportation examples ready with the numbers you actually own.

Sablecross Logistics, Director of Transportation. I've been in conversation with them 6 days, B+ fit, 78% match, ghost risk medium, no docs built. The recruiter admitted yesterday that the req isn't approved yet. Do I move this or leave it?

Hold in conversation. An unapproved req means there is nothing to apply to, so a move would be fiction. Six days is early and a B+ at 78% is worth keeping warm. Unlock: the recruiter confirms the req is approved — then build docs and go straight to applied.
Three Rivers Regional Authority / Chief Information Officer / sourced / 19 days / D / 31% / ghost risk high / no docs

sourced → rejected, and do not feel bad about it. A CIO seat is not adjacent to grants and contracts administration; a D fit at 31% is the system saying so plainly. Nineteen days of it sitting there untouched is your own answer.

company: Havenridge County Human Services Department
title: Senior Grants and Contracts Administrator
stage: researched, 5 days
fit: A
match: 93%
ghost risk: low
docs: ready

Apply. researched → applied — 93% is the highest match on this board, it is the same work at a senior grade, and the docs are done. Five days of research is more than enough for a county posting whose scope you already know.

Ridgeline Policy Collaborative, Manager, Research and Evaluation. Sourced, 10 days, B fit, 67% match, ghost risk medium, no docs. The posting closes in three weeks and there's no salary listed. I keep opening this one and closing it again.

Leave it in sourced. A B at 67% and medium ghost risk is not enough to spend research hours on while stronger grants rows are live. It earns a move to researched if nothing better lands before the posting closes, or if they publish a salary.

Windward Voyages | Vice President, Commercial | sourced | fit A- | 84% | 2 days in stage | ghost risk low | tailored docs no
Found it in the industry newsletter. Worth real work?

Yes - sourced → researched. An A- fit at 84% that is only two days old earns the research hour before anything else on the board. Read their last two itinerary launches, then decide between a conversation and a direct application.

company: Cinderpeak Resorts
title: Senior Vice President, Revenue and Distribution
stage: researched
fit: A
match: 91%
days in stage: 6
ghost risk: low
tailored docs: yes
note: their SVP of distribution accepted my connection request last night and replied "happy to chat"

A live "happy to chat" from the SVP outranks the portal. Move researched → conversation. At A fit and 91% you can afford to talk before you apply, and your docs are already tailored if the call turns into a referral.

Forwarding the gist: Sablewood Resorts Group came back on Director of Revenue Strategy - "moving forward with candidates whose experience is closer to hotel RevPAR." I was in applied, 19 days, B+ fit, 76% match, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

That is a stated fit reason, not a stall. applied → rejected. They named hotel RevPAR as the gap and a B+ at 76% was never going to close it. Nineteen days ending in a real sentence beats nineteen days of silence.

Windward Voyages made the offer on VP Commercial. Stage offer, 4 days, A- fit, 84% match. Comp is fine, but the role now reports two levels below where it was scoped during the interviews and they will not move it. I am saying no.

Then record it properly: offer → rejected, declined. The reporting line changed from what was scoped in the interviews, and comp being fine does not repair structure. Send the decline inside a day so the A- relationship stays warm for the next req.
Emberline Health, Clinical Content Lead - sourced, 11 days, fit D, match 41%, ghost risk high, no docs. I keep skipping past it.

You skip it because it is not yours. sourced → rejected. A D at 41% with no tailored docs after eleven days is a decision you have already made quietly; making it official clears the column for the analyst listings that actually fit.
Larkfield Health System / Healthcare Data Analyst / researched / A- / 88 / 5 days / low / docs yes

Stop researching. researched → applied, today. Docs are tailored, ghost risk is low, and an A- at 88 is the strongest lab-to-analyst bridge you have. Five days of reading is already more than this needs.

Verity Ridge Health Network wrote back about Clinical Informatics Analyst - recruiter Dana wants a 45-minute screen next week. I have been in applied 12 days, B+ fit, 79% match, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

Scheduling the screen is the transition. applied → interview. Twelve days of medium ghost risk just resolved in your favor, and at B+/79% the screen is where you translate bench work into informatics language. Confirm with Dana before you touch anything else.
quick one - Halewood Bancorp, Director, Regulatory Affairs. researched, sat 24 days, fit C, 58% match, ghost risk high, docs not tailored. i think i am keeping it out of guilt

You are. researched → rejected. Twenty-four days without tailoring a single document is the honest signal, and a C at 58% against high ghost risk never justified the writing time. Guilt is not a stage.

| company | title | stage | fit | match | days | ghost | docs |
| Stonebrook Credit | Director of Credit Risk | applied | A | 93% | 9 | low | yes |
Panel got scheduled for the 28th - four people, including the CRO.

applied → interview, and this is your best row: A fit, 93%, nine days, low ghost risk. A four-person panel with the CRO on it means loss-forecasting depth, not culture chat. Block prep time now; the rest of the board waits until the 28th.
Northfell Capital Partners, VP Risk Analytics. Interview stage, 16 days, A- fit, 87% match. Third round finished Tuesday and HR emailed asking for comp expectations and two references.

References plus a comp question after round three is the offer motion. interview → offer once the number arrives - send both references today so nothing waits on you. At A-/87% after sixteen days, answer with a range, not a single figure.

Ardent Ledger Group, Senior Manager, Model Risk Governance. I had been in conversation 21 days with the hiring manager. He wrote today that the req is being downleveled and refilled internally. Fit B, 71%, ghost risk medium, docs yes.

conversation → rejected. An internal refill after a downlevel closes the outside track no matter what your B at 71% says. The twenty-one days were not wasted - keep the hiring manager, because director reqs come back around.
Brightloom Foods just posted Continuous Improvement Manager. Sourced today, day 1, fit B+, 77%, ghost risk low, no docs yet. Do I bother?

Bother. sourced → researched. Day one with low ghost risk means the req is fresh and you are early, and B+/77% deserves an hour of reading before you spend an evening on documents. Research first, tailor second.

Palisade Converting - Production Manager, Packaging
stage: researched
fit: A
match: 90%
days: 8
ghost risk: low
docs: yes
I have reread the posting four times.

Four rereads is the tell. researched → applied. There is nothing left to learn: A fit, 90%, tailored docs, low ghost risk, eight days sitting still. Submit tonight and make the eighth day the last one.
Auto-rejection from Brightfield Provisions on Plant Manager, no reason given. Was in applied 31 days, B fit, 68% match, ghost risk high, docs tailored.

Log it: applied → rejected. Thirty-one days at high ghost risk usually ends exactly like this, and a B at 68% for a director-scope plant seat was a stretch worth testing once. No reason given means there is nothing here to fix.
Three Rivers Regional Authority is hiring a Chief Information Officer. Sourced, 3 days, fit A-, 85%, ghost risk low, docs no. Public postings like this usually close fast.

Then do not sit in sourced. Move it sourced → researched today. At A-/85% with low ghost risk the constraint is your untailored docs, not the fit - and the research pass is what tells you which of their systems to name when you write.

Kingsford County, Washington - Contracts and Procurement Manager. Two calls with their deputy director; she told me to go through the official portal because the county cannot hire off a referral. Stage conversation, 14 days, B+ fit, 74%, ghost risk medium, docs yes.

She just named the only path. conversation → applied - file through the portal this week and reply to her with the confirmation number. Fourteen days of conversation bought you an advocate, and at B+/74% her sponsorship inside the process is what moves the file.
Kingsford County pulled the Contracts and Procurement Manager offer - the council cut the position in the budget vote. I was at offer, 6 days, B+ fit, 74%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

offer → rejected, pulled. A budget vote removed the seat, so there is nothing to negotiate and nothing you did wrong in six days. Ask the deputy director whether it returns next fiscal cycle, then rebuild the top of the board this week.
Halvard & Pike, Senior Manager, Digital Merchandising - researched, 9 days, B+ fit, 80% match, ghost risk medium, docs yes. A former colleague of mine just started there as a merchandising director.

Use her before you use the portal. researched → conversation. A B+ at 80% is exactly the band where an internal voice changes the read, and nine days in researched has bought nothing. Ask what the team is missing, then apply with the docs you already have.
Wickham Row replied on Director of E-commerce Operations - "we would like you to meet the VP of Digital." Applied, 7 days, A- fit, 86%, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

applied → interview. Seven days to a VP-of-Digital meeting matches the low ghost risk and the A- at 86%. Treat it as a first round, not a casual chat - your tailored docs are the script, so reread what you claimed before the call.

Northmarch Consumer Brands, Category Manager, Marketplace. Second interview was two weeks ago, then nothing, and today: "we have filled the position." Interview stage, 18 days, B fit, 72%, ghost risk high, docs yes.

interview → rejected. Eighteen days at high ghost risk after round two was already the answer; the email only confirmed it. Ask the recruiter one question - what separated the hire - because at B/72% a specific gap is worth more to you than the rejection is.
Kettlebridge Depot, WMS Solutions Analyst. sourced · 4 days · A fit · 92% · ghost risk low · docs no

sourced → researched, and quickly. A 92% graded A is the highest thing on this board and it has been idle four days with nothing tailored. Read their fulfillment stack, then write once - a role this aligned deserves specifics, not a generic submission.
Tarnwell Logistics Group - Senior Manager, Fulfillment Systems. In researched 29 days. Fit C, 57%, ghost risk high, docs no. It is a step up in title and I keep talking myself out of it.

Your instinct is correct. researched → rejected. Twenty-nine days, a C at 57%, high ghost risk, and not one tailored document - four signals pointing the same way. A stretch title only works when the match is close, and 57% is not close.

Steadmoor Distribution, Continuous Improvement Analyst, Network Operations. Had a 30-minute intro with the ops director; he said the posting goes live Monday and to apply the day it does. conversation, 5 days, A- fit, 83%, ghost risk low, docs yes.

Monday is the move: conversation → applied. He gave you a date and a reason, which is as close to an invitation as this gets. With docs tailored and an A- at 83%, landing in the first handful of applicants on a fresh req is real advantage.
Rejection from Kettlebridge Depot on WMS Solutions Analyst - they went with someone who had direct experience on their WMS vendor. applied, 22 days, A fit, 92%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

applied → rejected, and this one stings because the A and the 92% were honest. The gap was their specific vendor, not your capability. Twenty-two days with a named reason is worth one reply asking whether a second req exists where vendor familiarity matters less.
Marrowlane Health, Vice President of Engineering. researched, 10 days, A- fit, 88%, ghost risk low, docs ready. I have 25 minutes before a board call.

Twenty-five minutes is plenty. researched → applied - docs are ready, the fit is A- at 88%, and ten days of reading already exceeds what a VP req warrants. Hit submit and spend the leftover minutes on the board call.

Basalt Systems, Senior Vice President, Engineering. Interview stage, 24 days, A fit, 94% match, ghost risk low. Finished the final loop plus a dinner with the CEO. Their head of talent asked about my comp structure and start-date flexibility.

Comp structure and start date in the same email is offer language. interview → offer. Twenty-four days through a full loop and a CEO dinner at A/94% does not end in a pass. Give the start date freely; give comp as a range you would sign.
Marrowlane Health passed after the panel: "strong leader, but we need someone who has scaled through a Series C." interview, 20 days, A- fit, 88%, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

interview → rejected. They named the bar, and a stage-of-company requirement does not bend for an A- at 88%. Twenty days, low ghost risk, and a real reason is a clean loss. Ask to stay in touch - VP engineering reqs recur at the same companies.
Beacon Halloway Events, Supplier Performance Manager. Sourced 16 days. Fit C, 54%, ghost risk high, no docs. Honestly I saved it because the company name sounded nice.
sourced → rejected, no ceremony. Sixteen days without opening a document says everything, and a C at 54% with high ghost risk is not a near miss. Company names are not a signal. The 54% is.

Vermilion Trail Journeys - Operations Manager, Guided Travel. researched, 7 days, A fit, 89%, ghost risk medium, docs yes. Their ops lead posted asking for guide-scheduling recommendations and I actually have an answer.

That opening will not come twice. researched → conversation - reply with the scheduling answer and attach nothing. At A/89% with medium ghost risk, being known before your application lands is worth more than the seven days you spent reading.
Saltmoor Expeditions moved me to a first-round interview on Director of Global Operations. applied 15 days, B+ fit, 81%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

applied → interview, fifteen days in, and the medium ghost risk just cleared. A B+ at 81% for a director title means your tailored docs did the work - reread them before the call so you defend the version they actually read.
Osterby Clinical Communications, Associate Medical Writer. Sourced, 2 days, fit B+, 76%, ghost risk low, docs no. Coming from hospital pharmacy this feels like the most realistic door.

Then open it properly: sourced → researched. Two days old with low ghost risk means you have room, and a B+ at 76% improves fast once your documents read as writing rather than dispensing. Research now, tailor after.

Emberline Health, Clinical Content Lead. Good call with their clinical director; she said the formal posting requires an application from everyone and pointed me at the req. conversation, 11 days, B+ fit, 79%, ghost risk low, docs yes.

conversation → applied. When the director points at the req, the conversation has done its job - eleven days converted into a warm referral and your docs are ready. Apply within forty-eight hours and tell her it is in, while she still remembers the call.
Osterby Clinical Communications, Associate Medical Writer - now in researched, 4 days, B+ fit, 76%, ghost risk low, docs no. Push it to applied?

Not yet - stay in researched. Applying with untailored docs under a pharmacist title wastes a B+ at 76%. The unlock is small: one writing sample plus a resume that leads with documentation work. Have both by Friday, then go straight to applied.
Prairiestone Credit Group posted Data Analyst I, Lending. sourced, 1 day, B fit, 70%, ghost risk low, docs no. I am a math teacher, so entry level is the plan.

Right plan, right stage: sourced → researched. Day one with low ghost risk is the best position any row can hold. A B at 70% for an entry title is workable, but only if research turns classroom analysis into lending vocabulary before you write anything.

| Cordwain Financial | Analyst, Portfolio Reporting | researched | D | 45% | 21 | high | no |
Worth a shot?
No. researched → rejected. A D at 45% after twenty-one days, high ghost risk, and no documents is not a long shot, it is a distraction. Spend that hour on a role you would grade above D.

Ashgrove Mutual Insurance, Associate Pricing Analyst. Applied 27 days ago, no human contact at any point, and the portal flipped to "no longer under consideration" this morning. B fit, 68%, ghost risk high, docs yes.

applied → rejected - the portal flip is the decision. Twenty-seven days without a human reply at high ghost risk was the pattern long before today. At B/68% with tailored docs and zero contact, the problem sits upstream: the resume is not reading as analyst yet.
Recruiter from Prairiestone Credit Group called - they want a technical screen on SQL for Data Analyst I, Lending. applied, 8 days, B fit, 70%, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

applied → interview. Eight days to a SQL screen is a genuine result for a career switch sitting at B/70%. That screen is the whole decision, so give this week to SQL and nothing else - low ghost risk means the rest of the board will keep.
Delderfield Foods / Controls Engineer / researched / A / 91% / 5 days / low ghost / docs yes. I keep opening this board and doing nothing.

So do one thing: researched → applied. An A at 91% with tailored docs and low ghost risk is not a decision, it is a submit button. Five days is the only weak number in that row, and it is the only one you control.
Halbrook Stamping Works wants me onsite for a shop-floor walkthrough plus interview. Maintenance and Automation Supervisor. applied, 11 days, B+ fit, 79%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

A walkthrough invitation is a stage change - applied → interview. Eleven days at medium ghost risk resolved well, and B+/79% for a first supervisor title means they are testing whether you can lead a floor. Talk to the technicians while you walk it.
Delderfield Foods called after the second interview. Going with an internal candidate. interview, 17 days, A fit, 91%, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

An internal candidate is not a loss you could have prevented. interview → rejected. Seventeen days, an A at 91%, two rounds and low ghost risk made you the outside finalist. Email the hiring manager and ask to be called first on the next controls req.

Fenwick Housing Institute - Senior Research Associate, Housing Policy. researched, 12 days, A- fit, 86%, ghost risk medium, docs yes. Someone I met at a housing conference last year runs their evaluation team.

You have a warm door and you are not using it. researched → conversation. Twelve days of medium ghost risk is time one email to the evaluation lead would have spent better. At A-/86% with docs ready, a conversation converts faster than the portal.
Ridgeline Policy Collaborative wrote: "the position has been placed on hold pending funding." Manager, Research and Evaluation. applied, 25 days, B+ fit, 80%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

A funding hold is not a maybe you can work. applied → rejected. Twenty-five days at medium ghost risk and now an indefinite pause - stop budgeting attention for it. Keep the recruiter's address; an 80% match with tailored docs restarts in a day if money returns.
Fenwick Housing Institute, Senior Research Associate, Housing Policy - researched, 3 days, A- fit, 86%, ghost risk medium, docs no. Apply now while it is fresh?

Hold in researched. An A- at 86% is too good to spend on an untailored resume, and three days is not late. Unlock condition: a publications-forward resume plus a cover paragraph on evaluation design. Once both exist, move it straight to applied.
Tallow Creek Market Group, Director of Field Operations. sourced, 19 days, fit C, 60% match, ghost risk high, docs no. It has been sitting at the top of my board doing nothing.

Clear it: sourced → rejected. Nineteen days at the top of the board with a C, 60%, high ghost risk and no documents means you have been avoiding it, not evaluating it. The board reads better once the first column is honest.
Marchand Outfitters, Vice President, Retail Operations. researched 9 days, A fit, 93%, ghost risk low, docs yes. Biggest title I have gone for.

Go. researched → applied. A 93% match graded A with tailored docs is the strongest row you will see this quarter, and low ghost risk says the process is real. Nine days is enough research for a VP req; title size is not a reason to wait.

Wickham Row. I had been in conversation with their COO about Director of E-commerce Operations. He wrote today that they are pausing all director hires until January. conversation, 28 days, B+ fit, 82%, ghost risk medium, docs yes.

Twenty-eight days ended by a freeze, not by you. conversation → rejected. A January pause is longer than your search can wait, and a B+ at 82% does not survive that gap on an active board. Diary a note to write the COO in January.

Marchand Outfitters - VP, Retail Operations. Interview, 21 days, A fit, 93%, ghost risk low. Four rounds done; the CEO's chief of staff emailed asking when I could speak with their head of people about "next steps and package."

"Package" is the word that changes the stage. interview → offer. Four rounds in twenty-one days at A/93% with low ghost risk, and the conversation is now compensation. Take the call quickly and have your number decided before it starts.
Junipero Parcel, Vice President, Last-Mile Delivery - researched, 7 days, A- fit, 87%, ghost risk low, docs yes. What is the move?
researched → applied, no further reading. Seven days, A-/87%, low ghost risk and finished docs is the definition of ready. More research only adds delay on a VP req whose pool will close early.
Update: Steadmoor Distribution set a panel for Continuous Improvement Analyst, Network Operations - three interviewers, next Thursday. applied, 10 days, B fit, 71%, ghost risk medium, docs tailored.

applied → interview. Ten days and a three-person panel means the medium ghost risk is gone. A B at 71% makes you the range candidate rather than the obvious one, so Thursday turns on one thing: a network problem you fixed and the number attached.
Junipero Parcel after the final round: "we have decided to move forward with another candidate." No further detail. interview, 23 days, A- fit, 87%, ghost risk low, docs tailored.

interview → rejected. A final-round loss with no detail after twenty-three days is worth one email asking what tipped it - at A-/87% you were close enough that the answer is usable. Then close the row and reinvest the prep hours.
